claim: Granite Speech 4.1 2B base model achieves word error rate of 5.33, the top score on Open ASR leaderboard, processing 4 minutes of audio per second of compute.
actionable: Compare Granite Speech 4.1 to other ASR models using Open ASR leaderboard metrics before deployment.

claim: Granite Speech 4.1 Plus model supports speaker attribution (diarization) and word-level timestamps but drops to 5 languages, removes translation features, and has higher word error rate than base model.
actionable: Choose Granite 4.1 Plus only if speaker diarization is required, accept the accuracy trade-offs.

claim: Granite Speech 4.1 2B NAR (non-autoregressive) model achieves real-time factor of 1,820 on H100 GPUs, enabling 1-hour audio transcription in ~2 seconds, without keyword biasing or speaker attribution.
actionable: Use NAR variant for high-throughput transcription when fine-grained control features are not needed.

claim: Flash Attention installation is required for non-autoregressive Granite models; on older GPUs like T4, installation may fail.
actionable: Verify Flash Attention compatibility before deploying NAR Granite models, especially on legacy GPU hardware.

claim: Keyword biasing in Granite Speech 4.1 base model allows passing domain-specific terms to improve transcription of technical names, acronyms, and special spellings.
actionable: Provide domain-specific keyword lists in the model prompt for domain-specific transcription tasks.

claim: Incremental decoding in Granite Speech 4.1 Plus enables passing previously transcribed text as prefix to continue from checkpoint, useful for long-form audio with speaker consistency.
actionable: Implement incremental decoding with overlapping chunks to maintain speaker labeling consistency across long podcast or meeting transcripts.

claim: IBM's Granite model suite includes vision (Docling), speech, embedding, and language models positioning it as comprehensive alternative to Phi models.
actionable: Evaluate Granite Vision for OCR/PDF extraction if using Granite Speech for consistency across model family.
